title: CAWS Working Spec
$comment: CAWSFIX-03: This schema is the single source of truth for working-spec structure. It replaces the deleted .caws/validate.js legacy validator. Every constraint that validator enforced is expressed here; no required field is silently dropped. Rationale for open decisions: (1) `additionalProperties: true` at the top level is intentional — agents legitimately add helpful extensions like `worktree:`, `waiver_ids:`, `status:`, `created_at:`, `type:` and we do not want to break existing specs. If tightening is desired in the future, audit .caws/specs/*.yaml first. (2) `contracts` is permitted to be an empty array: CAWSFIX-06 will later warn (not fail) when `mode=feature` + empty contracts; failing here would break CAWSFIX-05/06 specs that legitimately have `contracts: []`. (3) `change_budget` is forbidden per policy (CI enforces `.github/workflows/caws-gate.yml:42-55`): budgets come from `.caws/policy.yaml` risk tiers, not from specs.

not.$comment: change_budget is forbidden in working-spec.yaml: budgets are policy-driven, derived from risk_tier via .caws/policy.yaml. See .github/workflows/caws-gate.yml for the CI enforcement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the change. Format: uppercase/digit PREFIX segments separated by dashes, final segment is one+ digits (e.g. CAWSFIX-16, P03-TRUTH-001, ALG-001A-HARDEN-01). Matches SPEC_ID_PATTERN in spec-validation.js (CAWSFIX-21 alignment).

type.description: Type of work (informational; `mode` is the enforced gate input)

worktree.description: CAWS worktree name assigned to this spec

mode.description: Mode of change. `development` is the default used by `caws specs create`; feature/refactor/fix/doc/chore are the classic modes the legacy validator accepted.

waiver_ids.description: IDs of active waivers applying to this spec

modules.description: List of modules/paths the change touches

data_migration.description: Whether the change requires a data migration

invariants.description: Properties that must hold across the change (non-empty)

a11y.description: Accessibility requirements (may be empty array if not applicable)

security.description: Security requirements (may be empty array if not applicable)

contracts.description: API contracts. Empty array is permitted; CAWSFIX-06 will warn (not fail) when mode=feature with empty contracts.
